input.headline: I’ve been writing about housing for more than a decade, and I grew up and live in California. So I’ve seen how the state has struggled with its severe housing shortage.

raw.headline: I’ve been writing about housing for more than a decade, and I grew up and live in California. So I’ve seen how the state has struggled with its severe housing shortage.

winner: Why did the Californian house struggle with math problems? Because it was having a hard time adding more square feet!

candidates.text: Why did the California house ask for a date? Because it wanted to expand its family...but the plot of land said no.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific to housing and California, but the pun is weak and the setup feels forced. The humor lacks surprise and falls into a generic 'pun on house/land' trope."}

candidates.text: Why did the Californian house struggle with math problems? Because it was having a hard time adding more square feet!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific to the topic and uses a play on words with 'square feet' and math problems. However, it's slightly generic in its humor and lacks the sharp surprise or deeper connection to the housing shortage issue mentioned in the headline."}

candidates.text: Why don't houses in California ever feel lonely? Because they always have a family of tenants living inside!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific to the context of housing in California and uses a play on words with 'family of tenants.' However, it's a bit generic and lacks the surprise or depth that would make it stand out in a humor competition."}

candidates.text: Why don't houses ever get homesick in California? Because they're always coming back to their roots!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'coming back to their roots,' but it's a bit generic and doesn't strongly tie into the specific context of housing shortages in California."}

candidates.text: Why did the Californian housing shortage go on a diet? Because it wanted to shed some square feet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the pun on 'shed some square feet,' but it's a bit generic and lacks the specificity that could make it more surprising or impactful in the context of housing issues."}
